counter_case: The PR adds runtime_cache to the LangRuntimeContract Protocol, but existing test mocks (_FakeLang in test_ts_nextjs_framework.py and test_js_nextjs_framework.py) do not expose runtime_cache. The detection.py code gracefully degrades via getattr fallback, so these tests pass but silently skip the caching path entirely. This means the nextjs framework tests -- which are the primary consumers of the changed code -- never actually exercise runtime_cache storage or retrieval. Only the new integration test in test_scan_workflow_integration_direct.py covers the runtime_cache path. If anyone later removes the getattr fallback in detection.py (since the Protocol now formally requires runtime_cache), those test mocks would break. Additionally, the PR branch is based before the defensive json_default dataclass handler (61bb7cb3), so merging will require a rebase to avoid conflicts in the test and engine files.
summary: Correct root-cause fix. The bug is that workflow.py passes a reference to state['review_cache'] into LangRun, and framework detection writes non-serializable dataclass objects (EcosystemFrameworkDetection, NextjsFrameworkInfo) into that shared dict, causing a crash on JSON serialization. The fix introduces a separate runtime_cache field on LangRuntimeState that is never wired to persisted state, cleanly separating ephemeral memoization from persisted data. The approach is architecturally sound -- it draws the right boundary between scan-scoped caching and state persistence. Implementation is thorough: LangRuntimeState, LangRunOverrides, LangRuntimeContract protocol, accessors, and _LANG_OVERRIDE_DICT_FIELDS are all updated consistently. Both detection.py and nextjs.py are migrated. The new integration test directly proves the end-to-end contract (framework objects stay out of persisted state).
conditions: Update _FakeLang in test_ts_nextjs_framework.py and test_js_nextjs_framework.py to include runtime_cache={} so the existing nextjs framework tests exercise the actual caching path rather than silently degrading to uncached mode.
Rebase onto 0.9.11 HEAD to incorporate the defensive json_default handler (61bb7cb3) and the other two commits (1a0439e2, 18b497a2) that landed since this branch was forked.
implementation_notes: The fix is at the right layer and is the right solution. The runtime_cache field is properly threaded through all five touchpoints (LangRuntimeState dataclass, LangRunOverrides dataclass, LangRuntimeContract Protocol, LangRunStateAccessors property, _LANG_OVERRIDE_DICT_FIELDS frozenset). The detection.py refactor to extract _framework_runtime_cache() is a nice touch for DRY and safety. The getattr fallback in detection.py is intentionally tolerant of duck-typed lang objects, which aligns with the existing pattern in the codebase. The defensive json_default handler from 61bb7cb3 (on 0.9.11 but not this branch) provides belt-and-suspenders protection, so even after this PR merges, if any future code accidentally writes dataclasses to review_cache, the serializer won't crash.
